name: Engrove Tonearm Profile Designer
summary: Convert calibrated Engrove Manual Trace top and side views into an editable 3D tonearm loft, then analyze, refine and export it on a desktop browser.
description: A PC-only, browser-local tonearm engineering application that opens with an empty session, imports calibrated JSON, Engrove project packages and paired SVG exports from Engrove Manual Trace, constructs a deterministic centerline-and-ring 3D loft, and preserves the TD053F parametric, Freeform, physics, solver, AI handoff, session, report, PNG and STL workflows.
capabilities: Start with an empty workspace and create geometry only after an explicit import, session open or new-parametric action.
Import multiple Engrove Manual Trace JSON, .engrove-trace, .engrove-project and paired SVG files with per-file plane classification and provenance.
Construct a deterministic Freeform centerline-and-ring loft from calibrated orthographic top and side silhouette bounds and Manual Trace station data.
Preserve TD053F parametric Cobra and classic geometry controls, Freeform AI workbench, cartridge picker, solver, LT mechanism preview and physical analysis.
Save and reopen complete sessions and export STL, Onshape sidecar JSON, 3D PNG, AI Vibe JSON and technical Markdown reports.
Run entirely in the desktop browser without uploading operator files to an application server.

inputs.name: manual_trace_files
inputs.type: Engrove Manual Trace JSON, project package or paired SVG files
inputs.description: Calibrated orthographic trace artifacts used as deterministic geometry input.

inputs.name: tonearm_session
inputs.description: A previously saved complete application session.

inputs.name: operator_parameters
inputs.type: desktop form controls and validated AI response JSON
inputs.description: Explicit design, material, cartridge, solver and export settings.

outputs.name: tonearm_session
outputs.description: A resumable application session including active geometry and Manual Trace import provenance.

outputs.name: tonearm_mesh
outputs.description: The explicitly selected active parametric or accepted Freeform mesh.

outputs.name: technical_report
outputs.description: A runtime snapshot of controls, analysis, source audit and stated limitations.

outputs.name: visual_reference
outputs.description: An 800 by 600 pixel image of the active 3D model.

outputs.name: ai_and_onshape_sidecars
outputs.description: Structured AI Vibe and Onshape handoff metadata for the active design.

whenToUse: Use after producing calibrated top and side silhouettes in Engrove Manual Trace.
Use to refine and compare tonearm profile geometry on a desktop workstation.
Use when browser-local STL, session, image or technical-report output is needed.
whenNotToUse: Do not use an uncalibrated or axis-ambiguous trace as dimensional manufacturing truth.
Do not treat the analytical stiffness, resonance or manufacturability proxies as FEA, metrology or laboratory certification.
Do not use the tool as a replacement for final B-rep CAD cleanup, tolerancing or production validation.
limitations: A trace-derived loft requires at least one valid top-plane and one valid side-plane Manual Trace artifact with an origin, millimetre scale and usable contour geometry.
Top and side silhouettes constrain station width, height and center position; unmeasured cross-section shape between those bounds remains a deterministic modelling assumption.
The imported Freeform kernel clamps values to its documented geometric limits and blocks inputs that would require silent dimensional loss.
Three.js rendering and browser downloads require a current desktop browser with WebGL and local download permission.
STL is a mesh reference output, not a parametric STEP or manufacturing-ready B-rep model.

mayClaim: The tool starts with no active or rendered 3D design.
Supported Manual Trace files are parsed and converted locally in the browser after explicit operator action.
Accepted trace-derived geometry is bound to the existing Freeform render, session, report and export source path.
The original TD053F runtime modules and portable acceptance harnesses remain included in the source repository.
negativeClaimBoundaries: A source-level or local browser check is not proof of Cloudflare deployment or production availability.
Deterministic trace conversion does not establish that the source image, calibration or operator contour is correct.
A closed STL mesh and analytical proxy results do not prove manufacturability, structural performance or audio performance.
mustNotClaim: Do not claim metrology-grade dimensional accuracy without independently verified calibration and source imagery.
Do not claim CAD, FEA, Onshape, manufacturing or acoustic validation from this browser application alone.
Do not claim that paired SVG geometry overrides the Manual Trace JSON source-of-truth contract.

faq.question: Why does the tool open with an empty viewport?
faq.answer: A new session intentionally has no active design. Import calibrated Manual Trace files, open a saved session, or explicitly create a parametric design.

faq.question: Which Manual Trace files can I import?
faq.answer: The importer accepts Engrove Manual Trace JSON exports, resumable .engrove-trace or .engrove-project packages, and paired SVG exports containing the Engrove trace binding metadata.

faq.question: What is required to create a trace-derived 3D model?
faq.answer: Provide calibrated top and side views with finite millimetre scale, an engineering origin, non-ambiguous axes and usable silhouette contours. The importer blocks incomplete geometry instead of guessing silently.

faq.question: Does the imported model exactly reproduce every 3D surface?
faq.answer: No. Orthographic silhouettes determine longitudinal center, width and height at sampled stations. Cross-section family and interpolation between views remain explicit deterministic assumptions.
